city_square.name: The City Square
city_square.description: A tall column rises from the center of the square. Atop it stands the statue of the Happy Prince, covered in gold leaf and sapphires, overlooking the city. The square is grand but surrounded by narrow alleys where the poor live.

sewing_room.name: The Seamstress's Room
sewing_room.description: A cramped, dark room in a poor quarter. A young seamstress sits hunched over her work, embroidering passion-flowers on a satin gown for the Queen's next ball. Her sick child lies feverish on a bed in the corner.

garret.name: The Writer's Garret
garret.description: A cold attic under the eaves. A young playwright huddles over his desk, shivering, trying to finish a play for the theater manager. No fire has been lit; he is too cold to work.

balcony.name: The Governor's Balcony
balcony.description: An elegant marble balcony overlooking the city. A beautiful young lady stands here, gazing at the stars with the Professor of Ornithology beside her. The world is beautiful from up here, if you don't look down.

happy_prince.name: The Happy Prince
happy_prince.description: A gilded statue atop a tall column. Once a prince who lived in a palace where sorrow was not allowed to enter. Now, from his height, he sees all the misery of his city and weeps.

swallow.name: The Swallow
swallow.description: A small bird who fell in love with a beautiful reed and stayed behind when his friends migrated to Egypt. Now autumn is here, the reed will not speak to him, and Egypt waits.

seamstress.name: The Seamstress
seamstress.description: A poor working woman with a sick child. She embroiders passion-flowers for the Queen's gown while her boy lies burning with fever, asking for oranges.

playwright.name: The Playwright
playwright.description: A young writer trying to finish a play. He is cold, hungry, and the theater manager says he must deliver by morning or find another profession.

governors_daughter.name: The Governor's Daughter
governors_daughter.description: A beautiful young woman who walks on the balcony with the Professor of Ornithology. She speaks of stars and love, unaware of the suffering below.

professor.name: The Professor of Ornithology
professor.description: A learned man who walks with the Governor's daughter on the balcony, discoursing about the migration of birds while the swallow shivers below.

golden_statue.name: The Gilded Prince
golden_statue.description: A statue covered in fine gold leaf, with two bright sapphires for eyes and a large red ruby in his sword-hilt. Beautiful and sad.
golden_statue.symbolic_meaning: Beauty that sees suffering; wealth that can heal

column.name: The Tall Column
column.description: A very tall stone column rising from the center of the city square.

embroidery.name: Passion-Flower Embroidery
embroidery.description: An unfinished satin gown embroidered with passion-flowers for the Queen's next ball.
embroidery.symbolic_meaning: The irony of decorating a gown with symbols of suffering

gown.name: The Queen's Gown
gown.description: A magnificent satin gown awaiting its final embroidery.

manuscript.name: The Unfinished Play
manuscript.description: A half-finished manuscript for a dramatic play. The writer's hands are too cold to hold the pen.

canon_events.description: The Swallow takes shelter at the feet of the Happy Prince
The Happy Prince asks the Swallow to carry the ruby to the seamstress
The Swallow delivers the ruby to the seamstress's sick child
The Prince gives a sapphire to the freezing playwright
The Prince gives his second sapphire to the match-girl
The Swallow stays with the Prince and dies of cold
The Prince's lead heart breaks in two

custom.winter_is_coming: The cold intensifies as the story progresses
custom.wealth_can_heal: The Prince's gold and gems can relieve suffering
